items.fight_news: Chimaev makes his first title defense after dethroning Dricus du Plessis. He enters this main event completely undefeated at 15-0 and is looking to solidify his era at middleweight.
Strickland returns to New Jersey, hoping to repeat his upset magic against Israel Adesanya and regain his middleweight crown in front of a hostile crowd.
Former champion Robert Whittaker recently predicted Chimaev will run over Strickland due to his relentless wrestling, adding massive external pressure to Strickland's camp.

The hatred here is very real. After escalating threats and bad blood stemming from a gym dispute in Las Vegas, the UFC has reportedly ramped up security and limited their face-off interactions for fight week.
The co-main event is a brilliant clash of flyweight youth, with both men coming off spectacular wins over former champions to earn this title opportunity.
Van defends his newly acquired flyweight title for the first time against the undefeated Japanese sensation Taira, marking the first UFC title fight between competitors born in the 21st century.
Unlike the main event, Van and Taira are known for their deep mutual respect, ensuring a high-quality martial arts matchup built on pure competition rather than bad blood.

bouts.title: Alexander Volkov vs. Waldo Cortes Acosta

items.fight_news: Volkov brings massive height and reach advantages into this heavyweight collision against the notoriously heavy-handed Cortes Acosta.
Cortes Acosta relies heavily on his boxing background and legendary durability, aiming to push a frantic pace against the highly technical Russian veteran.
A definitive win for either man on this main card solidifies a top ranking and a potential number-one contender spot in a shifting heavyweight landscape.
Brady is a smothering grappler looking to bounce back with a statement win and prove he absolutely belongs in the upper echelon of the welterweight title conversation.
Buckley brings explosive, fight-ending knockout power and a growing highlight reel of striking finishes to the Octagon.
It is a quintessential striker-versus-grappler matchup where Buckley's takedown defense will be tested immediately from the opening seconds.
Two of the most experienced and battle-tested veterans in UFC history clash in a lightweight matchup guaranteed to be a crowd-pleaser.
Green's slick boxing and flashy hands-down style meet Stephens' legendary one-punch knockout power.
Expect an incredible amount of trash talk inside the Octagon as both of these fighters love to perform and put on a show for the live crowd.
Two hungry middleweight prospects look to make a violent statement on the prelims and break into the crowded top 15 conversation.
Both fighters are known for their finishing instincts, making this a strong candidate for Fight of the Night honors.
In one of the most anticipated promotional debuts of the year, former Bellator champion Yaroslav Amosov finally steps into the Octagon.
Amosov faces a massive welcome test in the towering Spanish finisher Joel Alvarez, promising fireworks for hardcore MMA fans.
A pivotal lightweight crossroads fight. Dawson looks to re-establish himself as a top contender, while the heavy-handed Rebecki aims to bounce back and claim a marquee name.
This matchup was specifically booked by matchmakers to see who truly belongs in the upper echelon of the 155-pound division.
The legendary Jim Miller extends his own UFC record for most fights, bringing his iconic submission game to New Jersey for a homecoming bout.
Miller faces fellow veteran and tri-state area favorite Jared Gordon, ensuring the Prudential Center crowd will be deafening for this lightweight clash.
Expect a striking masterclass in this middleweight prelim as Kopylov brings his lethal kickboxing resume against the aggressive Brazilian Tulio.
Neither man likes to take a backward step, practically guaranteeing an all-action stand-up war.
A classic international grappler-versus-striker dynamic as the Philadelphia native Sabatini tries to ground the evasive French kickboxer Gomis.
Sabatini will have the regional crowd advantage, but Gomis enters the cage with a wave of momentum behind his striking displays.
Russia meets Brazil in a stylistic matchup that promises high-level grappling exchanges and explosive transitions from the opening bell.
This bout highlights the global pipeline of talent currently flooding into the UFC's middleweight ranks.
Opening the night in Newark, two highly touted flyweight prospects look to set the arena on fire and start UFC 328 with a bang.
With both men eager to prove they are the future of the 125-pound division, the pace of this early prelim will be relentless.
